# Ranger Class Features — Player Core (Remaster)

Source: Player Core pg. 152
Class: Ranger
Key Attribute: Strength or Dexterity
Hit Points: 10 plus your Constitution modifier (per level)

---

## Class Progression Table

| Your Level | Class Features |
| --- | --- |
| 1 | Ancestry and background, attribute boosts, initial proficiencies, hunt prey, hunter's edge, ranger feat |
| 2 | Ranger feat, skill feat |
| 3 | General feat, will expertise, skill increase |
| 4 | Ranger feat, skill feat |
| 5 | Attribute boosts, ancestry feat, ranger weapon expertise, skill increase, trackless journey |
| 6 | Ranger feat, skill feat |
| 7 | General feat, natural reflexes, skill increase, perception mastery, weapon specialization |
| 8 | Ranger feat, skill feat |
| 9 | Ancestry feat, nature's edge, ranger expertise, skill increase |
| 10 | Attribute boosts, ranger feat, skill feat |
| 11 | General feat, warden's endurance, medium armor expertise, skill increase, unimpeded journey |
| 12 | Ranger feat, skill feat |
| 13 | Ancestry feat, skill increase, martial weapon mastery |
| 14 | Ranger feat, skill feat |
| 15 | Attribute boosts, general feat, greater natural reflexes, greater weapon specialization, perception legend, skill increase |
| 16 | Ranger feat, skill feat |
| 17 | Ancestry feat, masterful hunter, skill increase |
| 18 | Ranger feat, skill feat |
| 19 | General feat, medium armor mastery, skill increase, swift prey |
| 20 | Attribute boosts, ranger feat, skill feat |

---

## Initial Proficiencies

At 1st level, you gain the listed proficiency ranks in the following statistics. You are untrained in anything not listed unless you gain a better proficiency rank in some other way.

### Perception
- Expert in Perception

### Saving Throws
- Expert in Fortitude
- Expert in Reflex
- Trained in Will

### Skills
- Trained in Nature
- Trained in Survival
- Trained in a number of additional skills equal to 4 plus your Intelligence modifier

### Attacks
- Trained in simple weapons
- Trained in martial weapons
- Trained in unarmed attacks

### Defenses
- Trained in light armor
- Trained in medium armor
- Trained in unarmored defense

### Class DC
- Trained in ranger class DC

---

## Key Terms

**Flourish:** Actions with the flourish trait are special techniques that require too much exertion for you to perform frequently. You can use only 1 action with the flourish trait per turn.

**Press:** Actions with this trait allow you to follow up earlier attacks. An action with the press trait can be used only if you are currently affected by a multiple attack penalty. You can't use a press action when it's not your turn, even if you use the Ready activity. Some actions with the press trait also grant an effect on a failure. The effects that are added on a failure don't apply on a critical failure. If your press action succeeds, you can choose to apply the failure effect instead. (For example, you may wish to do this when an attack deals no damage due to resistance.)

**Warden Spells:** Your relationship with the world around you extends from the physical to the magical. Certain feats grant you warden spells, which are a type of focus spell. It costs 1 Focus Point to cast a focus spell. When you gain your first warden spell, you also gain a focus pool of 1 Focus Point. You refill your focus pool during your daily preparations, and you regain 1 Focus Point by spending 10 minutes using the Refocus activity to commune with nature. Your warden spells are primal spells. When you first gain a warden spell, you become trained in spell attacks and spell DCs. Your spellcasting attribute is Wisdom. Focus spells are automatically heightened to half your level rounded up. Certain feats give you more focus spells. The maximum Focus Points your focus pool can hold is equal to the number of focus spells you have, but it can never be more than 3 points.

---

## Level 1 Class Features

### Ancestry and Background
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** In addition to the abilities provided by your class at 1st level, you have the benefits of your selected ancestry and background.

### Attribute Boosts
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** In addition to what you get from your class at 1st level, you have four free boosts to different attribute modifiers. At 5th level and every 5 levels thereafter, you get four free boosts to different attribute modifiers. If an attribute modifier is already +4 or higher, it takes two boosts to increase it; you get a partial boost, and must boost that attribute again at a later level to increase it by 1.

### Initial Proficiencies
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 1st level, you gain a number of proficiencies that represent your basic training, noted at the start of this class.

### Hunt Prey
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** 1 action [one-action]
- **Traits:** Concentrate, Ranger
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 154
- **Description:** When you focus your attention on a single foe, you become unstoppable in your pursuit. You gain the Hunt Prey action.

  **Hunt Prey** [one-action] — You designate a single creature as your prey and focus your attacks against that creature. You must be able to see or hear the prey, or you must be tracking the prey during exploration. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Perception checks when you Seek your prey and a +2 circumstance bonus to Survival checks when you Track your prey. You also ignore the penalty for making ranged attacks within your second range increment against the prey you're hunting.

  You can have only one creature designated as your prey at a time. If you use Hunt Prey against a creature when you already have a creature designated, the prior creature loses the designation and the new prey gains the designation. Your designation lasts until your next daily preparations.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This single action sets up all of the ranger's combat advantages. The +2 circumstance bonus to Seek and Track is substantial. Ignoring the second range increment penalty is a significant advantage for bow rangers. The designation persists until daily preparations, so you don't lose it between encounters unless you reassign it.

### Hunter's Edge
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 154
- **Description:** You have trained to become a skilled hunter and tracker, gaining an extra benefit when you Hunt Prey depending on the focus of your training. Choose a hunter's edge. See the full Hunter's Edge options below.

### Ranger Feats
- **Level:** 1
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 1st level and every even-numbered level thereafter, you gain a ranger class feat.

---

## Hunter's Edge Options

### Flurry
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 154
- **Description:** You have trained to unleash a devastating flurry of attacks upon your prey. Your multiple attack penalty for attacks against your hunted prey is –3 (–2 with an agile attack) on your second attack of the turn instead of –5, and –6 (–4 with an agile attack) on your third or subsequent attack of the turn, instead of –10.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is the best edge for a ranger who wants to make many Strikes per turn. The MAP reduction is significant: at –3/–6 (or –2/–4 with agile) vs the normal –5/–10, your subsequent attacks are much more likely to hit. Pairs exceptionally well with Twin Takedown and other multi-attack feats.

#### Flurry — Masterful Hunter Upgrade (Level 17)
- **Description:** You can blend your weapon mastery with skillful targeting to make a series of precise attacks. If you have master proficiency with your weapon, your multiple attack penalty for attacks against your hunted prey is –2 (–1 with an agile weapon) on your second attack of the turn, and –4 (–2 with an agile weapon) on your third and subsequent attacks of the turn.
- **Mechanics Notes:** At level 17 with master proficiency, the MAP becomes –2/–4 (or –1/–2 with agile). With an agile weapon, the third attack is only at –2, which is nearly as accurate as most characters' second attack. This makes Flurry rangers among the best at sustained multi-attack damage.

---

### Outwit
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 154
- **Description:** You are talented at outwitting and evading your prey. You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to Deception checks, Intimidation checks, Stealth checks, and any checks to Recall Knowledge about the prey, and a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against your prey's attacks.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Outwit is the most versatile edge, providing defensive and skill benefits. The +1 AC against prey is always active and stacks with nothing else that gives circumstance AC (like a shield). The +2 to Deception, Intimidation, Stealth, and Recall Knowledge is excellent for skill-focused combat maneuvers and identifying enemy weaknesses.

#### Outwit — Masterful Hunter Upgrade (Level 17)
- **Description:** Your mastery of skills allows you to overwhelm your prey. If you have master proficiency in Deception, Intimidation, Stealth, or the skill you use to Recall Knowledge about your prey, increase the circumstance bonus against the prey with that skill from +2 to +4. If you have master proficiency with your armor, increase the circumstance bonus to AC against the prey from +1 to +2.
- **Mechanics Notes:** The +4 circumstance bonus to skills against prey is very large — a +4 to Stealth against your hunted prey makes you extremely hard to detect when near them. The AC bonus going to +2 with master armor proficiency makes you significantly harder to hit by your prey.

---

### Precision
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 154
- **Description:** You have trained to aim for your prey's weak points. The first time you hit your hunted prey in a round, you also deal 1d8 additional precision damage. (Precision damage increases the damage you already deal, using the same type, but is ineffective against creatures that lack vital organs or weak points.) At 11th level, the extra damage increases to 2d8 precision damage, and at 19th level, the extra damage increases to 3d8 precision damage.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Precision is the best edge for damage-per-hit builds, especially with bows. The extra damage only applies on the first hit per round, so it rewards accuracy over volume. Precision damage is ineffective against oozes, constructs, and similar creatures. Damage scales: 1d8 (level 1), 2d8 (level 11), 3d8 (level 19).

#### Precision — Masterful Hunter Upgrade (Level 17)
- **Description:** Your weapon mastery allows you to hit your prey's vital areas multiple times. The second time in a round you hit your hunted prey, you also deal 1d8 precision damage. At 19th level, your second hit in a round against your hunted prey deals 2d8 precision damage, and your third hit in a round against your hunted prey deals 1d8 precision damage.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This dramatically increases Precision's damage output. At level 17, a Precision ranger deals 2d8 on first hit and 1d8 on second hit. At level 19, it becomes 3d8 on first hit, 2d8 on second hit, and 1d8 on third hit — a massive boost to sustained DPR.

---

## Level 2 Class Features

### Skill Feats
- **Level:** 2
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 2nd level and every 2 levels thereafter, you gain a skill feat. You must be trained or better in the corresponding skill to select a skill feat.

---

## Level 3 Class Features

### General Feats
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 3rd level and every 4 levels thereafter, you gain a general feat.

### Skill Increases
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** At 3rd level and every 2 levels thereafter, you gain a skill increase. You can use this increase to either become trained in one skill you're untrained in or become an expert in one skill in which you're already trained. At 7th level, you can use skill increases to become a master in a skill in which you're already an expert, and at 15th level, you can use them to become legendary in a skill in which you're already a master.

### Will Expertise
- **Level:** 3
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 154
- **Description:** Your mental defenses grow stronger. Your proficiency rank for Will saves increases to expert.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Upgrades Will from trained to expert at level 3. This is the ranger's only Will save improvement (no master upgrade later), so Will remains a relative weakness compared to Fortitude and Reflex.

---

## Level 5 Class Features

### Ancestry Feats
- **Level:** 5
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Description:** In addition to the ancestry feat you started with, you gain an ancestry feat at 5th level and every 4 levels thereafter.

### Ranger Weapon Expertise
- **Level:** 5
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 154
- **Description:** You gain every advantage from your weapons. Your proficiency rank for martial weapons, simple weapons, and unarmed attacks increases to expert. You gain access to the critical specialization effects of all such weapons and unarmed attacks when attacking your hunted prey.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Expert proficiency increases your attack modifier by +2 compared to trained. Critical specialization effects only trigger against your hunted prey — this is a key limitation. For example, a bow's critical specialization (pin target, reducing Speed by 10 feet) only applies when you crit your hunted prey.

### Trackless Journey
- **Level:** 5
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 154
- **Description:** When you move through natural terrains, you are hard to track. You always gain the benefits of the Cover Tracks action in such terrains, without moving at half your Speed.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Normally Cover Tracks requires moving at half Speed. This lets you automatically cover your tracks in natural terrain at full Speed. Only works in natural terrains — not in urban or manufactured environments.

---

## Level 7 Class Features

### Natural Reflexes
- **Level:** 7
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 154
- **Description:** You've honed your reflexes dodging falling trees and pounding hail. Your proficiency rank for Reflex saves increases to master. When you roll a success on a Reflex save, you get a critical success instead.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is the ranger's "Evasion" equivalent. Master Reflex + success-to-crit-success means you almost never take full damage from Reflex-based effects (like Fireball). This is a tremendously powerful defensive feature.

### Perception Mastery
- **Level:** 7
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 154
- **Description:** You have highly developed acuity and attention to detail. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to master.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Advances Perception from expert (initial) to master. This gives the ranger one of the best Perception progressions in the game, eventually reaching legendary at level 15.

### Weapon Specialization
- **Level:** 7
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** You inflict greater injuries with the weapons you know best. You deal 2 additional damage with weapons and unarmed attacks in which you're an expert. This damage increases to 3 if you're a master, and 4 if you're legendary.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Flat bonus damage based on proficiency rank. At level 7 (expert), this is +2. At level 13 (master from Martial Weapon Mastery), damage increases to +3. This stacks with other damage bonuses and applies to every hit.

---

## Level 9 Class Features

### Nature's Edge
- **Level:** 9
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** You always find the weak points in your foes' defenses when they're on unfavorable terrain. Enemies are off-guard to you if they're in difficult terrain.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Off-guard imposes a –2 circumstance penalty to AC. This applies to ALL enemies in difficult terrain, not just your hunted prey. Extremely powerful in outdoor encounters or when combined with abilities that create difficult terrain (such as Snare Crafting or ally spells).

### Ranger Expertise
- **Level:** 9
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** You've gained techniques that improve your cunning and skill. Your proficiency rank for your ranger DC increases to expert. If you have warden spells, your proficiency rank for spell attacks and spell DCs increases to expert.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Improves ranger class DC to expert, affecting class abilities that use the ranger DC (like certain feat effects). If you've taken warden spell feats, this also improves your spell attack and spell DC.

---

## Level 11 Class Features

### Warden's Endurance
- **Level:** 11
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** You shake off a grizzly's grab or a poison's ill effects with equal ease. Your proficiency rank for Fortitude saves increases to master. When you roll a success on a Fortitude save, you get a critical success instead.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is the ranger's "Juggernaut" equivalent. Master Fortitude + success-to-crit-success means you almost never suffer the full effects of Fortitude-based effects (like poison, disease, or paralysis). Combined with Natural Reflexes at level 7, the ranger now has two master saves with the success-to-crit upgrade.

### Medium Armor Expertise
- **Level:** 11
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** You've learned to defend yourself better against attacks. Your proficiency ranks for light armor, medium armor, and unarmored defense increase to expert.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Expert armor proficiency adds +2 to AC compared to trained. This applies to light, medium, and unarmored defense simultaneously.

### Unimpeded Journey
- **Level:** 11
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** You move quickly through obstacles, whether they're tumbled stone or tangled undergrowth. You can ignore the effects of difficult terrain. As normal for ignoring difficult terrain, this also lets you treat the hindrances of greater difficult terrain as those of difficult terrain.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is the ranger's "Wild Stride" equivalent. Ignoring difficult terrain means you move at full Speed through rubble, undergrowth, snow, etc. Greater difficult terrain is treated as normal difficult terrain (costs 1 extra foot per foot instead of 2 extra). This pairs extremely well with Nature's Edge — you move freely through difficult terrain that makes enemies off-guard to you.

---

## Level 13 Class Features

### Martial Weapon Mastery
- **Level:** 13
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** You fully understand the intricacies of weapons. Your proficiency ranks for unarmed attacks, simple weapons, and martial weapons increase to master.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Master weapon proficiency increases your attack modifier by +2 over expert. Also increases Weapon Specialization damage from +2 to +3. This is the highest weapon proficiency the ranger reaches (no legendary upgrade).

---

## Level 15 Class Features

### Greater Natural Reflexes
- **Level:** 15
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** You avoid explosions with reflexes a cat would envy. Your proficiency rank for Reflex saves increases to legendary. When you roll a critical failure on a Reflex save, you get a failure instead. When you fail a Reflex save against an effect that deals damage, you halve the damage you take.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is the ranger's "Improved Evasion" equivalent. Legendary Reflex + crit-fail-to-fail + half damage on fail makes you extraordinarily resilient against Reflex saves. Combined with success-to-crit-success from Natural Reflexes, the only way you take full damage from a Reflex save is on a critical failure (which is downgraded to a failure), and even then you take half. The ranger has one of the best Reflex save progressions in the game.

### Greater Weapon Specialization
- **Level:** 15
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** Your damage from weapon specialization increases to 4 with weapons and unarmed attacks in which you're an expert, 6 if you're a master, and 8 if you're legendary.
- **Mechanics Notes:** At level 15 with master proficiency (from Martial Weapon Mastery at 13), this gives +6 flat damage per hit. A significant increase from the +3 you had previously. This flat bonus applies to every hit.

### Perception Legend
- **Level:** 15
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** You notice the almost imperceptible. Your proficiency rank for Perception increases to legendary.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Legendary Perception is one of the rarest and most powerful proficiencies in the game. Very few classes reach legendary Perception — the ranger and rogue are among the few. This means extremely high initiative (if using Perception for initiative) and near-impossible to sneak past. Combined with the +2 (or +4 at level 17) to Seek hunted prey, the ranger is the best at finding hidden enemies.

---

## Level 17 Class Features

### Masterful Hunter
- **Level:** 17
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** You have honed your abilities as a hunter to incredible levels. Your proficiency rank for your ranger class DC increases to master. If you have warden spells, your proficiency rank for spell attacks and spell DCs increases to master. When using a ranged weapon that you have master proficiency in, you can ignore the penalty if attacking your hunted prey within the weapon's second and third range increments.

  If you have master proficiency in Perception, you gain a +4 circumstance bonus to Perception checks when you Seek your prey, and if you have master proficiency in Survival, you gain a +4 circumstance bonus to Survival checks when you Track your prey.

  You also gain an additional benefit depending on your hunter's edge (see the Masterful Hunter Upgrade for each edge above).
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is a massive capstone-style upgrade at level 17. The range increment improvement (now ignoring penalties for 2nd AND 3rd range increments) is huge for bow rangers. The Seek/Track bonuses jump from +2 to +4 if you have master proficiency (which you have in Perception from level 7 and can have in Survival via skill increases). The hunter's edge upgrades (detailed above) significantly enhance each edge's core benefit.

---

## Level 19 Class Features

### Medium Armor Mastery
- **Level:** 19
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** Your skill with light and medium armor improves, increasing your ability to avoid blows. Your proficiency ranks for light and medium armor, as well as for unarmored defense, increase to master.
- **Mechanics Notes:** Master armor proficiency adds another +2 to AC over expert. This is the ranger's final armor upgrade. Combined with Outwit's AC bonus (if applicable), rangers can achieve respectable AC at high levels.

### Swift Prey
- **Level:** 19
- **Action Cost:** Passive
- **Traits:** —
- **Source:** Player Core pg. 155
- **Description:** You size up your prey with a glance. You can use Hunt Prey as a free action if it's your first action of your turn.
- **Mechanics Notes:** This is one of the ranger's most impactful features. Normally Hunt Prey costs 1 action, which is a significant action tax, especially when switching targets. Making it a free action means you can designate prey and still have all 3 actions for Strikes, movement, or other activities. This effectively removes the ranger's biggest action economy weakness.

---

## Proficiency Progression Summary

| Feature | Initial (Lv 1) | Mid (Lv 5–9) | High (Lv 11–13) | Apex (Lv 15–19) |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| Perception | Expert | Expert → Master (7) | Master | Legendary (15) |
| Fortitude | Expert | Expert | Master (11) | Master |
| Reflex | Expert | Expert → Master (7) | Master | Legendary (15) |
| Will | Trained | Trained → Expert (3) | Expert | Expert |
| Weapons (simple/martial/unarmed) | Trained | Expert (5) | Master (13) | Master |
| Light/Medium Armor & Unarmored | Trained | Trained | Expert (11) | Master (19) |
| Ranger Class DC | Trained | Trained → Expert (9) | Expert | Master (17) |
